risk_title: Infrastructure Destruction from Fuel Depot Explosion
risk_summary: December 2023 fuel depot explosion in Conakry destroyed schools and administrative buildings, necessitating emergency response and CERC activation to rehabilitate critical infrastructure.
evidence_quote: The Contingent Emergency Response Component (CERC) of the project in the amount of US$50 million was activated on June 15, 2024 to rehabilitate schools and administrative buildings that were destroyed during the December 2023 explosion of the fuel depot in Conakry

risk_title: Shock-Related Financing Gap and Project Replenishment Needs
risk_summary: CERC activation to address the fuel depot explosion has created a financing gap requiring additional financing to sustain project implementation and emergency response capacity.
evidence_quote: The World Bank and ANIES have also started preparing an additional financing to replenish the financing gap following the CERC activation.

risk_title: High Political and Governance Risk Rating
risk_summary: Project faces persistent high-level political and governance risks that could affect implementation stability and government commitment to social protection objectives.
evidence_quote: Political and Governance [Risk Rating]: High [at Last Approved Rating ISR Seq. 07 - 24 Jun 2024]
